metadata.preview:
# Auth refresh queue rationale
session_id: auth_refresh_session
title: Auth refresh queue rationale

## message auth_fact_1
The rejected design was one global mutex around all auth refresh work.

## message auth_fact_2
The global mutex was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other.

## message auth_fact_3
RefreshQueue uses a per-tenant key: same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce, while different tenants continue in parallel.

parts.text: {"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts deduplicates refresh work per tenant so duplicate refreshes for the same tenant coalesce, while refreshes for different tenants can continue in parallel rather than being serialized behind one global mutex.","evidence":{"session_id":"auth_refresh_session","blob_id":"auth_refresh_session.md","message_id":"auth_fact_3"},"rationale":"The relevant auth rationale states the implemented RefreshQueue behavior: per-tenant keys coalesce same-tenant duplicate refreshes while allowing different tenants to proceed in parallel. The rejected global mutex matters because it would have blocked unrelated tenants; this is consistent with the cited per-tenant rationale rather than a competing correction.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing retry queue rationale","Markdown parser onboarding cleanup","auth_queue_rationale_decoy_*"]}
